mission.title: Install Kubeflow Trainer for PyTorch on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kubeflow Trainer (formerly PyTorch Operator) is the Kubernetes-native way to run distributed PyTorch training jobs. It provides the TrainJob CRD and pre-built runtimes for PyTorch, enabling multi-node, multi-GPU training across a cluster.

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.description:
You need:
- A Kubernetes cluster v1.28+ (GPU nodes recommended for training workloads)
- `kubectl` configured to reach your cluster
- `helm` v3.8+ for OCI chart support

Verify your cluster:
```bash
kubectl version --short
kubectl get nodes
```

steps.title: Install Kubeflow Trainer with Helm
steps.description:
Kubeflow Trainer v2.2.0 ships as a single Helm chart that installs both the control plane and the built-in runtimes (PyTorch, XGBoost, JAX, MPI):

```bash
helm install kubeflow-trainer oci://ghcr.io/kubeflow/charts/kubeflow-trainer \
    --namespace kubeflow-system \
    --create-namespace \
    --version 2.2.0 \
    --set runtimes.defaultEnabled=true
```

This deploys:
- The Trainer controller manager
- TrainJob and Runtime CRDs
- Built-in training runtimes for PyTorch, XGBoost, JAX, and MPI/Flux

steps.title: Wait for the controller to be ready
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl rollout status deployment/kubeflow-trainer-controller-manager \
    -n kubeflow-system

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system
```

You should see the controller manager pod in `Running` state.

steps.title: Verify the CRDs are installed
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get crds | grep trainer.kubeflow.org
```

Expected output includes:
```
clustertrainingruntimes.trainer.kubeflow.org
trainingruntimes.trainer.kubeflow.org
trainjobs.trainer.kubeflow.org
```

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow Python SDK
steps.description:
The Kubeflow SDK lets you create and manage TrainJobs from Python:

```bash
pip install kubeflow
```

Verify:
```bash
python -c "import kubeflow; print(kubeflow.__version__)"
```

steps.title: Run a distributed PyTorch training job
steps.description:
Create a TrainJob that runs a simple distributed PyTorch training script across two workers:

```yaml
apiVersion: trainer.kubeflow.org/v1alpha1
kind: TrainJob
metadata:
  name: pytorch-distributed-example
  namespace: default
spec:
  runtimeRef:
    name: torch-distributed
  trainer:
    image: pytorch/pytorch:2.5.1-cuda12.4-cudnn9-runtime
    command:
      - torchrun
      - --nproc_per_node=1
      - -c
      - |
        import torch.distributed as dist
        dist.init_process_group(backend='gloo')
        rank = dist.get_rank()
        world_size = dist.get_world_size()
        print(f'Hello from rank {rank} of {world_size}')
        dist.destroy_process_group()
    numNodes: 2
```

Apply it:
```bash
kubectl apply -f pytorch-job.yaml
```

steps.title: Monitor the training job
steps.description:
```bash
# Check job status
kubectl get trainjob pytorch-distributed-example

# Watch pods
kubectl get pods -l trainer.kubeflow.org/trainjob-name=pytorch-distributed-example

# View logs from the coordinator node
kubectl logs -l trainer.kubeflow.org/trainjob-name=pytorch-distributed-example \
    -c trainer --tail=50
```

The job completes when all worker pods finish successfully and `STATUS` shows `Succeeded`.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow Trainer
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall kubeflow-trainer -n kubeflow-system
kubectl delete namespace kubeflow-system
```
